headline: March was literally the wildest month for AI ever—Claude 4.6 dropped w/ a 1M token window, Anthropic leaked its next model, and now we're all wondering if agents just became too real.

picks.name: Perplexity Computer
picks.tagline: AI agent orchestrator that codes, researches, and manages tasks across apps
picks.summary: Originally an internal Slack tool, it performed $776M in labor-equivalent work for subscribers. Agents can coordinate Claude Code and GPT Codex to handle multi-step workflows automatically.
picks.bestFor: Teams running complex workflows who need agents to coordinate work across tools

picks.name: Claude 4.6 Opus
picks.tagline: First Opus-class model w/ 1M token context window and 128K output
picks.summary: Lowkey the technical leader now with 1M context in beta. Agent Teams for parallel task coordination, adaptive thinking controls. 59% of devs prefer Sonnet 4.6 over the last version.
picks.tryIt: Try now via Claude API and claude.ai
picks.bestFor: Developers handling massive codebases and complex multi-step reasoning tasks

picks.name: Pika Video Chat Skill
picks.tagline: Real-time video & voice for AI agents powered by PikaStream1.0 model
picks.summary: Any AI agent can now integrate live video chat—agents get a face and remember personality across convos. Marks the shift from text-only toward actual embodied AI interactions.
picks.tryIt: Available for compatible AI agents in beta
picks.bestFor: Teams building customer-facing agents that need human-like real-time communication

picks.name: Anthropic Compliance API
picks.tagline: Audit logs API for enterprises, launched March 30
picks.summary: Admins get programmatic access to org-wide activity tracking. Built for regulated industries needing to log everything except model inference. Consolidates admin + resource activity in one feed.
picks.bestFor: Finance, healthcare, legal orgs that need strict compliance audit trails

watchNext.title: Claude Code crossed 8% of worldwide GitHub commits
watchNext.why: Fr this is insane—coding agents just became mainstream infrastructure. If your team isn't using Claude Code yet, you're literally leaving productivity on the table.

watchNext.title: OpenAI killed Sora app (6 months, $1B Disney deal gone)
watchNext.why: Tbh the lesson here is brutal—even massive companies can't sustain AI tools that don't hit product-market fit. Worth reading why it failed so fast.
